# Feature Definition Language (FDL)

> Define what your software should do as YAML blueprints. Let any AI generate the complete implementation for any framework.

## What is FDL?

FDL is a framework-agnostic specification system for defining software features. Instead of asking AI to "build login" and getting inconsistent results, you write (or extract) a blueprint that specifies every field, rule, outcome, error, and event — then any AI tool generates a correct, complete implementation for your chosen language and framework.

Blueprints are not templates. They are captured architectural expertise. A login blueprint teaches AI how to build rate limiting, token lifecycle management, and enumeration prevention for anything. A payment blueprint teaches asynchronous request-callback flows, idempotency, and state machines that apply to any distributed system.

## Repository

- URL: https://github.com/TheunsBarnardt/claude-fdl
- Documentation: https://theunsbarnardt.github.io/claude-fdl/
- JSON API: https://theunsbarnardt.github.io/claude-fdl/api/registry.json
- License: MIT
- Language: YAML blueprints + Node.js tooling
- AI Integration: Claude Code skills (7 slash commands), works with any AI tool

## Static API for AI Tools

Every blueprint is available as JSON (no scraping needed):
- Registry: https://theunsbarnardt.github.io/claude-fdl/api/registry.json
- Per-blueprint: https://theunsbarnardt.github.io/claude-fdl/api/blueprints/{category}/{feature}.json
- Example: https://theunsbarnardt.github.io/claude-fdl/api/blueprints/auth/login.json

## Seven Commands

- `/fdl-build` — Build a full app from a plain-English description. Searches existing blueprints, suggests related features via interactive checklist, disambiguates overlapping options, resolves gaps (delegates to create/extract skills), and orchestrates multi-blueprint code generation with cross-feature integration. The flagship command.
- `/fdl-create` — Create a blueprint from a plain-language conversation
- `/fdl-extract` — Extract rules from a document (PDF, DOCX, images) into a blueprint
- `/fdl-extract-web` — Crawl a documentation website and extract API operations into blueprints
- `/fdl-extract-code` — Analyze an existing codebase (local folder or git repo) and reverse-engineer features into blueprints
- `/fdl-extract-code-feature` — Scan a large repo, select specific features, extract only those as portable blueprints
- `/fdl-generate` — Generate implementation code from a blueprint for any language or framework

## 22 Included Blueprints

### Auth Pack — Security Patterns
- `login` — Email/password authentication. Patterns: scoped rate limiting, JWT token lifecycle (access + refresh rotation), account lockout, enumeration prevention, constant-time comparison.
- `signup` — User registration. Patterns: layered validation (format then uniqueness then business rules), bot detection (honeypot + CAPTCHA + rate limit), async email side effects.
- `password-reset` — Two-step recovery via email token. Patterns: two-phase async verification, token hashing in storage, session invalidation on credential change.
- `logout` — Session termination. Patterns: scoped token revocation (single device vs all), CSRF protection on state-changing actions.
- `email-verification` — Confirm email ownership. Patterns: token-based claim verification (reusable for phone, domain, webhook), rate-limited resends.

### Integration Pack — Distributed System Patterns
- `palm-vein` — Hardware SDK integration (PVD300/PVM310). Patterns: hardware device state machine (init/open/idle/operating/closed), buffer lifecycle, operation cancellation with cleanup.
- `biometric-auth` — Palm vein as login alternative. Patterns: multi-enrollment with selection, graceful hardware fallback, template auto-update on match.
- `chp-inbound-payments` — Receive payments from clearing system. Patterns: 1-second ACK + async processing, idempotency via UETR, request-response decoupling.
- `chp-outbound-payments` — Send payments through clearing. Patterns: bulk operations (submit N, track individually), conflict detection (HTTP 409), status polling.
- `chp-request-to-pay` — PayShap request-to-pay. Patterns: time-limited offers with expiry, cancellation with state guards, refund with sensible defaults.
- `chp-eft` — Batch electronic funds transfer. Patterns: settlement batching, reason code enums, post-fact correction of committed transactions.
- `chp-account-management` — Account and proxy management. Patterns: proxy resolution (indirect to direct identifier), pre-flight verification before expensive operations.
- `blockradar-api` — Blockchain wallet and transaction management. Patterns: multi-chain abstraction, webhook event catalog.

### UI Pack — Component Architecture and Tooling Patterns
- `shadcn-cli` — CLI for component registry distribution. Patterns: registry/plugin architecture (namespace + URL + auth), recursive dependency resolution, MCP server for AI discovery, framework detection, safe file mutation with backup.
- `shadcn-components` — 56 accessible React UI components. Patterns: CVA variant system, compound components, Radix Slot pattern (asChild), multi-theme architecture (6 styles, light/dark, OKLCH), focus management and ARIA compliance.

### Workflow Pack — Business Process Patterns
- `expense-approval` — Multi-step approval workflow. Patterns: role-based approval routing with thresholds, state machine with SLA enforcement and escalation, conditional field requirements, audit trail.

### Wealth Management Pack — Financial Services Patterns
- `portfolio-management` — Investment portfolio valuations and reporting. Patterns: multi-account hierarchies, real-time and delayed pricing strategies, FX conversion with audit trails, position weighting calculations, portfolio movement tracking, export to Excel.
- `market-data-feeds` — Real-time market pricing and data distribution. Patterns: multi-provider data aggregation (Iress, Marble, Bloomberg), quote caching strategies (1-min to 1-day TTL), async trade feed ingestion, watchlist management, intraday vs EOD pricing, index/commodity/forex data aggregation.
- `document-management` — Document storage, retrieval, and lifecycle. Patterns: SharePoint integration with metadata, soft delete audit trails, dynamic PDF generation from templates, one-time download URLs (InfoSlips), expiry enforcement, folder-based access control, permission inheritance.

### Onboarding Pack — Multi-Step Workflow Patterns
- `client-onboarding` — Multi-step client registration and profile completion. Patterns: role-based access (Client, PM, PM Assistant, Onboarding Officer), state machine with 11 states (initiated → mandate_signed), inline form validation, progress tracking, email notifications, SLA enforcement, duplicate detection.
- `advisor-onboarding` — Advisor/financial advisor registration and approval workflow. Patterns: identity verification with duplicate detection, CID approval gate, external integration (DocuSign for eSignature), 30-day SLA completion window, event-driven notifications, transactional record creation.
- `proposals-quotations` — Investment proposal and quotation generation and acceptance. Patterns: document upload and PDF generation, client decision workflow (accept/decline), state machine (pending → accepted/declined/expired), direct onboarding integration, event-driven document management, email notifications.

## Transferable Patterns Across Blueprints

These patterns appear across multiple blueprints and transfer to any domain:

- **Rate limiting** — login, signup, password-reset, payments. Apply to any brute-forceable endpoint or resource.
- **State machines** — auth flows, payment lifecycles, expense approval, sidebar UI. Apply to any entity with a lifecycle.
- **Token-based verification** — login, password-reset, email-verification. Apply to any claim verification (phone, domain, device pairing).
- **Idempotency** — all payment blueprints. Apply to any distributed system where duplicates can arrive.
- **Async callback patterns** — all CHP payment blueprints. Apply to any webhook-based integration.
- **Registry/plugin architecture** — shadcn-cli. Apply to building your own package registry, CDN, plugin marketplace.
- **MCP server integration** — shadcn-cli. Apply to making any catalog AI-discoverable.

## Combining Blueprints

Blueprints compose. Examples of what you can build by combining:

- Plugin marketplace with approval: shadcn-cli (registry) + expense-approval (state machine) + login (rate limiting)
- Payment gateway: chp-outbound-payments (orchestration) + login (API key auth) + chp-account-management (verification)
- SaaS onboarding wizard: signup (account creation) + email-verification (confirm ownership) + expense-approval (step workflow pattern)
- IoT device management: palm-vein (hardware state machine) + shadcn-cli (registry for device drivers) + chp-account-management (proxy resolution)
- Wealth management platform: portfolio-management (data) + market-data-feeds (integration) + document-management (storage) + login (authentication) + expense-approval (rebalancing workflows)
- Complete wealth onboarding system: client-onboarding (workflow) + advisor-onboarding (workflow) + proposals-quotations (data) + login (authentication) + email-notification (event notifications)

## Key Files

- Schema: schema/blueprint.schema.yaml
- Validator: scripts/validate.js
- Blueprints: blueprints/{category}/{feature}.blueprint.yaml
- Skills: .claude/skills/{skill-name}/SKILL.md

## Blueprint Structure

Required: feature, version, description, category, rules, and at least one of outcomes or flows.
Optional: fields, tags, related, events, errors, actors, states, sla, ui_hints, extensions.

Outcomes use given/then/result format with structured conditions (field, source, operator, value) and structured side effects (set_field, emit_event, transition_state, notify, call_service, create_record, delete_record).

## Supported Frameworks

All of them. Blueprints are framework-agnostic. Tested with: Next.js, Express, Laravel, Angular, React, Vue, C#/.NET, Rust, Python/Django, Go, Ruby on Rails, Flutter, Swift.

## Why Blueprints Matter for Next-Gen AI Models

As AI models become more agentic and autonomous (planning multi-step actions, executing across systems without human input at each stage), structured specifications become critical infrastructure. Blueprints give autonomous agents a complete contract — every field, rule, outcome, and error — so they implement exactly what's defined instead of guessing requirements. The better the model, the more it benefits from precise specifications. Blueprints are the interface between human intent and AI execution.
